Background

About Me

I am an Assistant Lecturer at the Military Technical College (MTC), Cairo, Egypt, in the Department of Computer Engineering and Artificial Intelligence. My work sits at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity in autonomous systems.

My proposed Ph.D. research focuses on cybersecurity through cognitive AI in autonomous systems — encompassing AI-driven intrusion detection, adaptive and integrated system security, and building resilience into safety-critical environments.

I hold an M.Sc. in Electrical Engineering with a thesis on Malicious Behavior Detection in Vehicular Adhoc Network Using Machine Learning , and a B.Sc. graduated with Excellent with Honor (GPA 3.786).

I speak Arabic natively, English proficiently, and have a basic command of Mandarin Chinese. I am passionate about teaching, research, and advancing the field of intelligent secure systems.

Work

Research Interests

🛡
AI-Driven Intrusion Detection
Developing intelligent detection mechanisms that leverage machine learning to identify and respond to threats in real time across networked autonomous systems.
🚗
Vehicular Network Security
M.Sc. research on detecting malicious behavior in vehicular communication networks — ensuring safety-critical vehicle-to-vehicle and infrastructure communications.
🧠
Cognitive AI in Cyber Defense
Exploring cognitive computing paradigms that enable autonomous agents to reason about, adapt to, and counter sophisticated cyber threats dynamically.
⚙️
Safety-Critical Systems Resilience
Enhancing the resilience of systems where failure is not an option — integrating layered security architectures with adaptive response strategies.
🦠
AI for Medical Diagnostics
Contributed (2020–2023) to AI-based COVID-19 detection frameworks — from data collection and model simulation to integration in intelligent diagnostic systems.
🔗
Adaptive Integrated Security
Researching holistic security architectures that adapt to evolving threat landscapes, combining policy-based management with AI-driven anomaly response.
